title: draba corrugata var. saxosa
intro: Draba corrugata var. saxosa, also known as Sax's draba, is a rare, small flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to high-altitude regions in Colorado, USA. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Sax's draba can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, but more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and herbal preparations.
